title: System Constraints Awareness

content.question: Which constraint are you ignoring that's capping your output?
sop: Map your business as a straight line from lead to delivered result.
At each step, note current throughput, how many units it can process per day (see calculation, page 5).
Identify the step with the lowest throughput number, that's your real constraint, not the one that feels most broken.
Redirect this month's entire improvement budget to that one step and ignore the rest.
Once throughput there rises, re-map the whole line, the constraint has likely moved.
questions: Which constraint are you ignoring that's capping your output?
If you fixed your biggest bottleneck this month, would revenue actually move, or is something else quietly capping you first?
Are you polishing the parts of your business that already work while one broken link limits everything downstream?

definitions.def: The slowest or most constrained step in a system.
The rate of output per unit of time.
Every system has a single constraint that caps output.

content.equation_example: Limits are each function's capacity in units/day: Sales can generate 50 leads/day, Onboarding can process 20/day, Fulfillment can ship 35/day. Output equals the minimum of those, 20/day. Higher means your weakest step's capacity is rising and total output rises with it; lower means one function drags the whole system down no matter how good the others are.
content.closing_statement: Your weakest link sets your true ceiling.

caption:
Principle Nº 167: System Constraints Awareness

Know your limits.

Ask yourself: Which constraint are you ignoring that's capping your output?
